concerns.text: FR-010 requires thermal conductivity from peer-reviewed literature or NIST only, but Constitution Principle VII permits 'Materials Project thermal properties endpoint OR NIST'. This creates a source conflict between spec and constitution.

Data model includes 'vif' column in merged_perovskite.csv, but spec FR-008 describes VIF calculation as a post-descriptor step for regression filtering. VIF should not be a required column in the canonical merged dataset.

Data model stores 'temperature' as original measurement temperature, but FR-013 requires normalization to 300K ±10K before analysis. The schema should clarify whether this column holds pre-normalization or post-normalization values.

Schema enforces vif ≤5 as a hard constraint, but FR-008 states VIF>5 descriptors are excluded from regression. The constraint should apply only to descriptors used in regression, not all rows in the dataset.

Constitution Check table claims all 7 principles satisfied, but Principle VII (Dataset Provenance) conflicts with FR-010 on thermal conductivity source. This check should be revised to reflect the actual source requirements.
